other_observers: Brandon Percival; Brandon Percival found the first bird, the one in alternate plumage and later found a second bird, one in basic plumage nearby but never side by side. It came from upriver and may not have been present when the first bird was found.
specific_location: Lake Pueblo State Park near the dam and best seen from the sailboard launch area
description: Two birds - one report  Alternate bird - This was a slender and seemingly short- necked, smallish loon (as compared to the nearby Common Loon) with very narrow delicate (for a loon) upturned bill. From a great distance this bird seemed to be uniformly medium to dark gray with a noticeable reddish throat when seen in just the right light. The bird was identfied mostly by its profile as described above. The bill was thin, short and dark.  Basic bird - This was also a slender and short-necked smallish loon as compared with Western Grebes nearby. The narrow upturned bill was easy to see, even from a great distance. The bird had a whitish throat and light gray nape that contrasted with the darker gray back and dark bill. Also noticeable where whitish flanks that contrasted with the darker back coloration. Both birds were often seen low in the water compared to the large grebes, even when they were loafing with their heads tucked.Behaviors: Both birds spent a lot of time on the surface loafing and the alternate plumaged bird spent a large amount of time with its head tucked. It did dive on three occassions.Call: none heard although I played a recording of song for attempted response but got none.Plumage: one basic and one alternate
